label: Clipboard: Copy selection or send SIGINT
description: With a terminal selection, copy to the system clipboard and clear; otherwise send a normal `Ctrl+c` to the pane.

label: Clipboard: Copy selection (strict)
description: With a terminal selection, copy and clear; otherwise forward `Ctrl+Shift+c` to the pane.

label: Clipboard: Paste (smart)
description: In hybrid-wsl ask the Windows native helper for the live clipboard at paste time; fall back to plain clipboard paste; paste exported WSL image path for images.

label: Clipboard: Paste (plain)
description: Force a normal clipboard paste without the image-export helper.

label: Link: Open URL from viewport
description: Trigger WezTerm QuickSelect to label every `http(s)://…` URL across the visible pane and WezTerm scrollback; press the label key to open the URL via the system default handler. Keyboard-first counterpart to `Ctrl+LeftClick`.

label: Pane: Split vertically
description: Create a top/bottom tmux split in the current pane.

palette.failure_message: Failed to create a vertical split.

label: Pane: Split horizontally
description: Create a left/right tmux split in the current pane.

palette.failure_message: Failed to create a horizontal split.

label: Pane: Rotate focus to next
description: Rotate focus to the next pane in the current tmux window (same semantics as tmux `prefix o`).

label: Pane: Close current
description: Run tmux `kill-pane` on the focused pane; if it is the last pane in the window, tmux closes the window too.

label: Tab: Next
description: Activate the next WezTerm tab in the current workspace.

label: Tab: Previous
description: Activate the previous WezTerm tab in the current workspace.

label: Tab: Jump by index
description: `Alt+N` activates the WezTerm tab at position N (1-9) in the current workspace.

label: Command chord: Activate prefix
description: Forward `Ctrl+k` to a tmux-backed pane so the follow-up chord keys (`v` / `h` / `x` / `g`) resolve in the tmux `command-chord` table; toast when the current pane is not running tmux.

label: Command palette: Open
description: Open the tmux-owned searchable command palette (falls back to WezTerm native palette outside tmux).

label: Command palette: Open WezTerm native
description: Open WezTerm's built-in command palette directly.

label: VS Code: Open current directory
description: In tmux-backed panes forward to tmux; in hybrid-wsl outside tmux open the worktree root on the Windows host.

label: VS Code: Force close all windows
description: Run `taskkill /IM code.exe /F` on the Windows host.

palette.confirm_message: Force close all VS Code windows? Unsaved editor state will be lost.
palette.failure_message: Failed to close VS Code windows.

label: Worktree: Pick in this repo family
description: Open a centered tmux popup worktree picker; selecting an unopened worktree creates its tmux window on demand.

label: Worktree: Cycle to next
description: Cycle to the next git worktree in the current repo family, creating its tmux window on demand when needed.

label: Worktree: Quick-create dev-* (long-lived)
description: Open a `dev-:` prompt; entered name becomes worktree `dev-<slug>` for long-lived parallel development. Agent uses the `<base>-resume` profile so cwd context is preserved across days.

label: Worktree: Quick-create task-* (short-lived)
description: Open a `task-:` prompt; entered name becomes worktree `task-<slug>` for PR-scoped focused work. Agent uses the `<base>-resume` profile.

label: Worktree: Quick-create hotfix-* (urgent)
description: Open a `hotfix-:` prompt; entered name becomes worktree `hotfix-<slug>` for urgent fixes. Agent uses the bare `<base>` profile so context is fresh — no stale resume state.

label: Worktree: Reclaim current task-/hotfix- worktree
description: Reclaim the current pane's worktree after merge. Refuses on main, dev-*, dirty/untracked changes, or unmerged branch. Switches focus to the main worktree window, runs reclaim detached, then closes the original window.

label: Chrome: Open debug profile
description: Open the configured Chrome debug browser profile from `wezterm-x/local/constants.lua` via the Windows native helper.

label: Attention: Jump to next waiting task
description: Jump to the next agent pane whose status is `waiting`. Repeated presses cycle past the current target; silent when nothing is waiting. Toasts outside tmux.

label: Attention: Jump to next done task
description: Jump to the next agent pane whose status is `done`; the entry is auto-cleared as the jump lands. Silent when nothing is done.

label: Attention: Open pending-task overlay
description: Open a centered InputSelector listing every pending agent task across workspaces. Fuzzy-filter and `Enter` to jump; pick the trailing `clear all` sentinel to wipe state.json. Works from any pane; final jump still needs tmux.

label: Session: Refresh current window
description: Respawn only the focused tmux window in place.

palette.failure_message: Failed to refresh current tmux window.

label: Session: Refresh current session
description: Rebuild the attached tmux session via replacement session.

palette.failure_message: Failed to refresh current tmux session.

label: Session: Refresh current workspace
description: Rebuild every tmux session that belongs to this workspace.

palette.confirm_message: Refresh every tmux session in the current workspace?
palette.failure_message: Failed to refresh current workspace sessions.

label: Session: Refresh all sessions
description: Rebuild every tmux session on the current tmux server.

palette.confirm_message: Refresh every tmux session on this tmux server?
palette.failure_message: Failed to refresh all tmux sessions.

label: Workspace: Switch to default
description: Switch to the built-in WezTerm `default` workspace.

label: Workspace: Switch to work
description: Open or switch to `work` using directories configured in `wezterm-x/local/workspaces.lua`.

label: Workspace: Switch to config
description: Open or switch to the `config` workspace.

label: Workspace: Cycle to next
description: Rotate through all currently known workspaces.

label: Workspace: Close current
description: Open a centered WezTerm confirmation overlay to close the current non-default workspace.

label: App: Quit WezTerm
description: Quit WezTerm and close all windows; WezTerm handles its own confirmation.
